refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency metric and its impact on safety-critical teleoperation, making it measurable and verifiable.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link to support teleoperation.
FR2: Ensure timely processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback with minimal delay to the tele-operator.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use a dedicated low-latency network protocol optimized for teleoperation.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes within the NGHE solution to process commands locally.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation data traffic in the network stack and vehicle communication systems.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Implement edge computing nodes within the NGHE solution to process commands locally.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between edge computing and vehicle control systems
coupling_check.notes: DP2 reduces latency by local processing, minimizing dependency on network variability, thus lowering coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Assumes edge computing nodes can be integrated within NGHE solution hardware/software.
Risk of increased system complexity and maintenance overhead.
Assumes network link meets baseline reliability to support telemetry.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor integration complexity and validate edge node performance early in development.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for processing teleoperation commands, managing local edge computing, and interfacing with vehicle control and network.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles bidirectional communication between tele-operator and NGHE solution for command and telemetry data.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends real-time vehicle status and sensor data to tele-operator.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-level task commands from NGHE Solution for execution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of teleoperation logic and edge computing; low diffusion as functionality is centralized.
Vehicle module contains control hardware but teleoperation processing is external, causing moderate diffusion.
NGHE Solution oversees system but teleoperation latency logic is better localized in Teleoperation module.
Safety Services monitors alerts but does not implement teleoperation latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing teleoperation latency control and edge computing in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if Teleoperation module becomes too complex.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure modular design within Teleoperation to separate edge computing and communication handling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (edge computing nodes) directly addresses the latency FR with measurable impact and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Concentration of teleoperation logic in one module may increase complexity but reduces cross-module diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows high containment score and low diffusion, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end via telemetry and network monitoring tools within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear mapping from FR to DP to IM with explicit interfaces and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Refine Teleoperation module internal architecture to manage complexity.
Validate network link performance assumptions early.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting safety and latency requirements. Verification and ownership are well defined.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link to support teleoperation.
FR2: Ensure timely processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback with minimal delay to the tele-operator.

revise.why: Current level is consistent and complete; next step is to decompose sub-FRs further.

assumptions: Network link meets baseline reliability and latency conditions.
Edge computing nodes can be integrated within Teleoperation module.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under operational network conditions.
Performance benchmarks of edge computing nodes.
